HOA pool season prep — the real checklist.

Most communities open the pool on hope. RISE communities open on a 32-point readiness checklist completed by May 15.

✦ Mechanical and electrical inspection signed off
✦ Lifeguard + amenity vendor contracts on file
✦ Pool plant: filtration, chemical balance, ADA access
✦ Compliance signage at every entry
✦ Resident communication scheduled — hours, rules, reservations
✦ Emergency shutoff and incident-response procedures documented

Every property. Every May. That is the difference between reactive and disciplined management.

Want a RISE summer readiness review for your community? letsrise@riseamg.com

#TogetherWeRISE #TexasHOA #HOAManagement

Summer in a high-rise is won in March.

By June, every system in the building — chillers, elevators, pool plant, garage ventilation, common-area HVAC — is being tested at full load. Boards that wait until summer are already behind.

The communities that thrive treat summer readiness as an operating discipline:
→ Mechanical PMs scheduled in Q1, completed by April
→ Reserve study reviewed before peak-use season
→ Vendor contracts re-bid and signed before they are needed
→ Resident communication on a standing monthly cadence

The cost of weak management does not show up on a fee invoice. It shows up in your reserve study three years later — when a chiller you should have rebuilt becomes a chiller you have to replace.

If you sit on a high-rise condo board and want a second opinion on your summer plan, send me a message.

#HOAManagement #TexasHOA #HighRiseLiving #TogetherWeRISE

Pre-season pool inspection in motion 🌊

Filtration. Chemical balance. ADA access. Decking. Compliance signage. Every box checked before a single resident swipes in.

This is what it looks like behind the gate at a RISE-managed community in May.

DM us "SUMMER" for our full 32-point amenity readiness checklist.

The proof is in the building.

Boards do not hire a management company so residents notice the management company. They hire one so residents notice that everything works.

This is what RISE residents are saying — unprompted, on Google and Birdeye — about the buildings we manage.

If your board is evaluating your current management partner, the question to ask is simple: when residents talk about the building, are they describing problems, or describing improvements?

Amenity rules that prevent problems this summer.

The communities with the smoothest summers are the ones that publish clear rules in May — not the ones that scramble to enforce them in July.

Our recommended baseline for every RISE-managed single-family community:

1. Pool access tied to current dues status — verified at the gate
2. Clubhouse reservations through one system, with a deposit policy
3. Guest limits posted at every amenity entry
4. ARC and modification rules linked from the homeowner portal
5. Compliance walks scheduled monthly through Labor Day

Adapted from our May blog. Save and share with your board.

#TogetherWeRISE #HOAManagement #TexasCommunities

Swipe → 5 things we check before flipping any RISE community to summer operations.

1. Mechanical readiness — pool plant, HVAC, common-area systems
2. Vendor confirmations — landscape, pool, pest, security
3. Compliance walk — signage, fencing, life-safety equipment
4. Resident communications — published, not improvised
5. Inspection sign-off — documented, not assumed

Whether it is a high-rise on the Coast or a master-planned community in Cypress, the checklist is the same.

The Marine Corps doesn't give you a checklist and wish you luck.

It builds a system — and then holds the line every day until the system becomes instinct.

That is exactly how we built RISE.

Same-day callbacks. Financials by the 15th. Monthly inspections. 24/365 emergency availability. These are not aspirations. They are operational standards we hold our team to, in every community we serve.

Our boards do not worry about whether their manager will show up. They know we will.

That's the RISE Difference.

#TogetherWeRISE #HOAManagement #RISEamg

Hurricane season begins June 1. You have twelve days.

Every Texas HOA and condo board — from a 4-story condo on the Bay to a 600-home master-planned community in Cypress — should be confirming the same five items this week.

✦ CC&Rs reviewed for emergency-access language and board authority
✦ Insurance coverage matched to current rebuild cost — not last year's figure
✦ 24/365 vendor list for water, roof, and tree on file
✦ Resident communication tree tested in the last 30 days
✦ Reserve fund exposure modeled for a worst-case deductible event

If even one of these is unconfirmed, you are not ready. RISE boards run this list every May.

If you would like a RiseShield insurance review before the season starts, message us at letsrise@riseamg.com.

#TogetherWeRISE #HurricanePrep #TexasHOA #RiseShield

Why we built RiseShield.

Most general carriers underwrite Texas HOA and condo associations against a generic multifamily template. Boards only find that out at claim time. By then, it is too late.

RiseShield is the master insurance program we built with Archer Risk Services for the way Texas associations actually live:

✦ Coverage matched to actual rebuild cost — not depreciated value
✦ Wind, named-storm, and water-damage clauses written for the Gulf Coast
✦ Claims response tied directly into RISE's 24/365 emergency operations
✦ Annual policy review built into our standard board reporting cycle

Heading into hurricane season, every Texas board should know exactly what their policy does and does not cover. We will pull your declarations and walk through it with you.

#RiseShield #TexasHOA #HurricanePrep

#RiseShield #TexasHOA #Condominiums #TogetherWeRISE

Swipe → 5 questions every Texas HOA board should ask about their insurance before June 1.

1. Is our policy written to current rebuild cost — or last year's?
2. Are wind and named-storm deductibles separated and disclosed?
3. Does our carrier understand high-rise / mid-rise / single-family differences?
4. Is there a documented claims-response protocol with our management team?
5. When was the last time the board reviewed declarations line-by-line?

If any of these is "we're not sure," that is the conversation to have this week — not after the first storm.

#TogetherWeRISE #RiseShield #HurricanePrep

Full financial transparency. By the 15th of every month.

That's not a bonus feature for RISE boards. It's the baseline.

Most management companies treat financial reporting like a quarterly courtesy. We treat it like the foundation of board accountability. Greg's experience is the standard, not the exception.

If your current management partner makes you wait for financial answers, that is not a service tier. It is a warning sign.

#TogetherWeRISE #HOAManagement #BoardLeadership

99%+ collections effectiveness — what that number actually protects.

For a 200-unit association with $4,800 annual dues, the difference between 92% (industry average) and 99% (RISE's standing benchmark) is roughly $67,200 every year that does not have to come out of reserves to cover delinquencies.

Compounded over a 5-year reserve cycle, that is more than $336,000 in preserved capital. Capital that can fund:
→ A roof replacement on schedule
→ An elevator modernization without a special assessment
→ A reserve study recommendation actually executed

Boards do not hire RISE because we are aggressive collectors. They hire RISE because the difference between a 92% and a 99% community is the difference between special assessments and stability.

#TogetherWeRISE #HOAManagement #TexasHOA
